_meta.purpose: Phase 4+ batch pipeline candidates — suitability spike results
_meta.scope: CAMS, ERA5, GFS. Not MVP. Runs in GitHub Actions cron, renders PNG → Cloudflare R2.
_meta.spike_method: Documentation review only — no accounts registered, no data downloaded.

auth.note: Separate from CDS — different URL despite sharing the cdsapi package. Each dataset requires manual Terms-of-Use acceptance via the web portal before API downloads work.

pm25.note: Derived from sum of organic matter, black carbon, sulfate, nitrate, sea salt, dust aerosol components — not a single-variable pull; request the composite variable directly.

cams.cadence: 2 forecast cycles/day at 00 UTC and 12 UTC; hourly timesteps at single-level
cams.queue_system: Queued asynchronous download via cdsapi; typical wait 5–30 min in off-peak, can exceed 2 h during peak demand

gh_actions.cron_description: Daily at 08 UTC — retrieves previous day 00 UTC cycle (available ~06 UTC)
gh_actions.rendering_lib: xarray + matplotlib (cartopy optional for projection) → PNG
gh_actions.timeout_risk: LOW — well within 6 h limit even with queue

landmines: ADS and CDS use different URLs despite sharing the cdsapi package — ~/.cdsapirc must point to https://ads.atmosphere.copernicus.eu/api for CAMS, NOT the CDS URL. Easy to ship wrong config.
Each dataset requires manual Terms-of-Use acceptance via browser BEFORE the API will return data — cdsapi gives cryptic 'Access restricted' errors without it.
PM2.5 is NOT a raw model variable — it is a diagnostic computed from 7 aerosol species. Request 'particulate_matter_d_less_than_2p5_um' directly; do NOT try to sum components.
cdsapi default retry is 3 attempts with no backoff — for long queues, wrap calls with tenacity or custom retry with exponential backoff.
CAMS forecast files: time dimension is indexed in seconds since epoch, not hours — xarray decode_times=True required or timestamps will be wrong.
GRIB2 files from CAMS use ECMWF parameter numbering, not standard WMO codes — cfgrib shortName may differ from NCEP conventions (e.g., 'pm2p5' not 'PM2.5').
GitHub Actions secret must be injected as env var CDS_API_KEY and written to ~/.cdsapirc in the job step — do not hardcode.

era5.full_name: ERA5 Monthly Averaged Data on Single Levels

auth.note: Different portal and URL from ADS/CAMS. Old format used UID:key pair — new format since 2024 uses a single personal access token. Scripts using the old format will silently fail with 403.

era5.cadence: Monthly means; new month available ~6th of following month

latency.era5t_preliminary: ~5 days after end of month (early release, may be revised)
latency.era5_final: ~2-3 months after ERA5T; corrections rare but possible
latency.note: For visualization, ERA5T (preliminary) is acceptable — use product_type='monthly_averaged_reanalysis' with year/month parameters

era5.file_format: GRIB1 (default for ERA5) or netCDF4 (specify format='netcdf' in request)

payload_size_estimates.note: Monthly means compress very well (~75% compression ratio). 1,038,240 cells × 4 bytes × 0.35 NC4 ratio.

era5.queue_system: CDS disk (fast) vs MARS tape (slow). Monthly means are on CDS disk — typical wait 2–10 min. Hourly ERA5 may require MARS tape (hours to days).

gh_actions.cron_description: Monthly on the 5th at 06 UTC — previous month ERA5T data reliably available by then
gh_actions.rendering_lib: xarray + matplotlib → PNG; use xr.open_dataset with engine='netcdf4'
gh_actions.timeout_risk: VERY LOW — monthly batch, short queue, small files

landmines: OLD ~/.cdsapirc format used 'url: ...' + 'key: UID:API_KEY' (colon-separated UID and key). New format (2024+) uses a single personal access token with no colon. Scripts written before 2024 will silently fail with 403.
Requesting hourly ERA5 for multiple months in one API call triggers MARS tape retrieval (hours to days of queue). Always request one month at a time for monthly-means pipeline.
format='netcdf' must be explicit in the cdsapi request dict — default is GRIB, and cfgrib is heavier to set up than netCDF4 for ERA5.
CDS requests have a 'maximum items' cap per request — requesting more than ~100,000 grid-points × time-steps may be split by the server, returning a zip of multiple files.

ERA5T (preliminary) vs ERA5 (final) are separate product_type values — 'monthly_averaged_reanalysis' for final, 'monthly_averaged_reanalysis_by_hour_of_day' for diurnal. Using wrong product_type silently returns wrong data.
CDS API returns a job ID for async requests — cdsapi client polls until done, but default timeout is none; set timeout= in Client() init to avoid hung GitHub Actions jobs.
Data is in K for temperature (not Celsius) and m for precipitation — normalize before colormapping or misleading renders result.

auth.note: Fully public, no registration required. Direct HTTP access to GRIB2 files.

gfs.client: cfgrib + xarray (pip install cfgrib xarray) or direct HTTP byte-range via requests + eccodes
gfs.latency_hours: ~3.5-4 h after cycle start (e.g., 00Z data available ~03:30-04:00 UTC)

payload_size_estimates.note: Verified: full f000 = 477 MB for 696 records. Per-record average 0.69 MB. Use NOMADS byte-range/filter service to pull only needed variables — avoid downloading full 477 MB.

gh_actions.cron_description: Every 6 h, 30 min after each cycle availability (00z→04:30, 06z→10:30, 12z→16:30, 18z→22:30 UTC)
gh_actions.rendering_lib: cfgrib + xarray for GRIB2 parse; matplotlib → PNG; eccodes system lib required in runner
gh_actions.timeout_risk: VERY LOW — no queue, small filtered payload

landmines: NOMADS 10-day rolling window: any URL older than ~10 days returns 404. Pipeline must use current date dynamically — never hardcode dates. Missing runs (e.g., delayed cycles) must be caught with HTTP 404 handling and fallback to prior cycle.
cfgrib requires the eccodes C library (not just the Python package) — on Ubuntu runners: 'sudo apt-get install libeccodes-dev'. Missing this causes a hard ImportError at runtime, not install time.

Full pgrb2 file is 477 MB — NEVER download the full file. Use the NOMADS filter service (filter_gfs_0p25.pl) with var_TMP=on&var_UGRD=on etc. to retrieve only needed variables. Filtered payload for 5 vars is ~4 MB.

cfgrib indexes GRIB2 files on first open, creating .idx sidecar files. On a read-only filesystem or tmpdir, this fails. Set indexing_time=None or use cfgrib.open_datasets() with index_path pointing to a writable temp dir.
GFS 00z cycle: available ~03:30 UTC but sometimes delayed to ~05:30 UTC during operational issues. Cron must include retry logic — check for file existence before processing.
Precipitation in GFS pgrb2 f000 is PRATE (instantaneous rate, kg/m2/s), NOT accumulated APCP. For accumulated precip, use f001-f006 with APCP. Ensure correct variable selection per forecast hour.
GFS grid is NOT centered at 0°: longitude range is 0–359.75°. For cartopy/matplotlib globe plots, roll the array to -180 to 180 with np.roll() or xr.Dataset.assign_coords() before plotting.

alternatives.numpy_pillow: Faster render (~3x) but requires manual colormap, no projection support. Acceptable for equirectangular frames.
alternatives.xarray_matplotlib: Recommended default — handles coordinate transforms, colormap, and missing data gracefully.

output_spec.color_model: RGB (no alpha — R2 CDN serves opaque tiles)

rendering_approach.upload: rclone or boto3 (s3-compatible) to Cloudflare R2

1.reason: No auth, no queue, 6-hourly cadence, verified file structure, simplest client (wget + cfgrib). Best for wind particles and precipitation. Implement first.

2.reason: PM2.5 and NO2 are differentiated vs GFS. Requires ADS account + ToU acceptance. Queue adds complexity but manageable. Implement second.

3.reason: Monthly cadence limits UI freshness. Valuable for climatology/anomaly context but lower urgency for Phase 4 launch. Implement third.
